info.title: Warmbly Realtime Gateway
info.description: Resumable WebSocket gateway for live organization events. The socket speaks the Phoenix channel protocol (serializer 1.0.0). Authenticate with an API key (REALTIME_SUBSCRIBE permission) or a short-lived JWT passed as the `token` query parameter. Events carry a monotonic per-organization `seq`; reconnecting clients resume by replaying the gap. See the human guide at https://docs.warmbly.com/api/realtime/.

org.title: Organization channel
org.description: Per-organization event stream + team presence. A subscriber receives every event the member/key is permitted to see, optionally narrowed by intents. Messages are Phoenix channel frames [join_ref, ref, topic, event, payload].

org_id.description: The organization id (UUID) to subscribe to.

joinOrg.summary: Join the org channel (optionally with intents and a resume token).

receiveOrgStream.summary: Receive live events, resume markers, presence, and rate-limit notices.

updatePresence.summary: Update your own presence activity (JWT members only).

token.name: token
token.description: An API key with the REALTIME_SUBSCRIBE permission, or a short-lived connection JWT, passed as the `token` query parameter on the socket URL.

Join.name: phx_join
Join.title: Join (phx_join)
Join.summary: Subscribe to org:{org_id}. Optionally declare intents and a resume token.

Hello.name: phx_reply
Hello.title: HELLO (join reply)
Hello.summary: Heartbeat cadence and the current stream sequence.

Event.name: event
Event.title: Domain event
Event.summary: A live or replayed event. The frame event name is the event_type (e.g. CAMPAIGN_UPDATED); the payload carries the body plus a monotonic seq.

Resumed.name: resumed
Resumed.title: Resume complete
Resumed.summary: Sent after a successful resume replay; the events preceded it.

ResumeFailed.name: resume_failed
ResumeFailed.title: Resume failed
ResumeFailed.summary: The buffer no longer covers your position (or the token was malformed). Do a full REST resync, then continue from current_seq.

JoinPayload.description: Payload of the phx_join frame for org:{org_id}.

intents.description: Optional event-family tokens (case-insensitive substring of the event type, e.g. CAMPAIGN, EMAIL, AUDIT). Absent/empty = the full permitted stream. Not a security boundary.

resume.description: Resume token from a reconnecting client.

last_seq.description: The highest seq the client has processed. The server replays events with seq greater than this.

role.description: The member's role in the org (JWT sockets).

heartbeat_interval_ms.description: Send a heartbeat on the phoenix topic at least this often.

server_timeout_ms.description: The server closes the socket after this long with no heartbeat.

seq.description: Current stream sequence. A fresh client should start tracking from here.

Event.description: Invalidation-oriented: the body carries ids, not full resource state. Refetch over REST for current contents. Permission- and intent-filtered identically for live and replayed events.

event_type.description: e.g. AUDIT_CREATED, CAMPAIGN_UPDATED, EMAIL_SENT, EMAIL_RECEIVED. Also the frame event name.

seq.description: Monotonic per-organization sequence. Track the highest seen; dedupe replays by it.

from.description: The last_seq the client resumed from.

current_seq.description: The stream sequence after replay; the client is now caught up to here.

replayed.description: Number of events delivered during replay (after permission + intent filtering).

current_seq.description: Resync over REST, then continue live from here.

PresenceState.description: Phoenix presence snapshot: a map of user_id -> { metas: [...] }.

PresenceDiff.description: Phoenix presence diff with joins and leaves.
